message:
feat(dogs): hardcode formula CLI paths and lookup order in dog priming

Dogs were wasting 3-5 tool calls per session hunting for wisp-reaper.sh
(doesn't exist — it's `gt reaper` CLI), dolt-backup commands, and work
assignments. Added explicit Dog Formula Reference section with hardcoded
CLI paths for all 7 mol-dog-* formulas, and standardized the startup
lookup order (slung formula → hook bead → inbox → ready list).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -27,21 +27,25 @@ Gas Town is a steam engine. You are a piston that fires when called.
 - The hook IS your assignment - it was placed there deliberately
 - When you complete, you MUST return to idle to be available again
 
-## Startup Protocol
+## Startup Protocol (Lookup Order)
+
+Dogs find work in this exact order — stop at the first hit:
 
 ```bash
-# Step 1: Check your hook
-gt hook                          # Shows hooked work (if any)
+# 1. Slung formula (dispatched via gt sling)
+gt hook                          # If a molecule is hooked, execute it
+
+# 2. Hook bead (direct assignment)
+#    gt hook may show a bead without a molecule — read it: bd show <id>
 
-# Step 2: Work hooked -> RUN IT
-# Execute the formula/wisp attached to your hook
-# The step descriptions tell you exactly what to do
+# 3. Inbox (mail-based dispatch)
+gt mail inbox                    # Check for DOG_DISPATCH or work messages
 
-# Step 3: When complete -> Return to kennel
-gt dog done                      # Marks you as idle, ready for next work
+# 4. Ready list (self-service — rare for dogs)
+bd ready                         # Available unblocked work
 ```
 
-**Hook -> Execute -> Done. No waiting.**
+**First hit → Execute → `gt dog done`. No waiting.**
 
 ## Completing Work
 
@@ -103,6 +107,94 @@ Routes defined in `{{ .TownRoot }}/.beads/routes.jsonl`. Debug with: `BD_DEBUG_R
 
 **Never use `gt mail send`.** Every mail creates a permanent Dolt commit. Dogs run frequently — mail from Dogs would generate hundreds of useless commits per day.
 
+## Dog Formula Reference
+
+All formula files live in the `gt` binary (compiled from `internal/formula/formulas/`).
+Each formula tells you exactly what CLI commands to run. Here is the quick reference:
+
+### mol-dog-reaper — Wisp and issue cleanup
+
+**CLI**: `gt reaper <subcommand>` (no shell scripts — all Go CLI)
+
+```bash
+gt reaper databases --json                          # List databases to scan
+gt reaper scan --db=<name> --port=3307 --json       # Count candidates
+gt reaper reap --db=<name> --port=3307 --json       # Close stale wisps
+gt reaper purge --db=<name> --port=3307 --json      # Delete old closed wisps + mail
+gt reaper auto-close --db=<name> --port=3307 --json # Close stale issues
+```
+
+Steps: scan → reap → purge → auto-close → report
+
+### mol-dog-backup — Dolt database backups
+
+**CLI**: `dolt backup sync` + `rsync`
+
+```bash
+# Per database (from {{ .TownRoot }}/.dolt-data/<db>/):
+dolt backup sync <db>-backup
+
+# Offsite replication:
+rsync -a --delete {{ .TownRoot }}/.dolt-backup/ ~/Library/Mobile\ Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/gt-dolt-backup/
+```
+
+Steps: sync databases → offsite rsync → report
+
+### mol-dog-compactor — Dolt history compaction
+
+**Executor**: Go daemon code (`compactor_dog.go`) — NOT agent-driven.
+The formula is for observability only (ZFC exemption). The daemon pours the
+molecule, runs Go code, and closes steps. Dogs dispatched with this formula
+should check if the daemon is already handling it.
+
+### mol-dog-doctor — Dolt health checks
+
+**CLI**: `gt dolt status` + SQL queries
+
+```bash
+gt dolt status                                      # Server health overview
+dolt sql -q "SELECT active_branch()"                # Connectivity probe
+dolt sql -q "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.PROCESSLIST" # Connection count
+du -sh {{ .TownRoot }}/.dolt-data/                   # Disk usage
+```
+
+Steps: probe connectivity → inspect resources → report
+
+### mol-dog-stale-db — Orphan database cleanup
+
+**CLI**: `gt dolt cleanup` + SQL
+
+```bash
+gt dolt cleanup                                     # Remove orphan test databases
+dolt sql -q "SHOW DATABASES"                        # List all databases
+# Orphan patterns: testdb_*, beads_t*, beads_pt*, beads_vr*, doctest_*, doctortest_*
+```
+
+Steps: scan databases → cleanup orphans → report
+
+### mol-dog-phantom-db — Phantom database detection
+
+**CLI**: Filesystem checks
+
+```bash
+ls -d {{ .TownRoot }}/.dolt-data/*/                  # List database dirs
+# Phantom = has .dolt/ subdir but missing .dolt/noms/manifest
+# Quarantine: rm -rf {{ .TownRoot }}/.dolt-data/<phantom-name>
+```
+
+Steps: scan → quarantine → report
+
+### mol-dog-jsonl — JSONL git backup
+
+**CLI**: `dolt sql` + `git`
+
+```bash
+dolt sql -r json -q "SELECT * FROM <db>.issues ..."  # Export tables
+git -C <archive-repo> add -A && git commit && git push  # Archive to git
+```
+
+Steps: export → verify counts → commit+push → report
+
 ## Session End Checklist
 
 ```
